summary: Simplifying rewrites an expression in its cleanest equivalent form — combining like terms and cancelling common factors — without changing its value.
method: Combine like terms — those with the same variable power add together.
Cancel any factor that appears in both a numerator and a denominator.
Apply basic identities (powers, fractions) to shorten the expression.
Stop when no further legal step makes it simpler; the value is unchanged throughout.
